_readme: HOW TO ADD A DISEASE: Add a new entry under 'diseases'. Give it a name, a list of symptom IDs (use snake_case), a severity (mild/moderate/severe/critical), and advice text. Any new symptom ID you use is automatically added to the model — just run 'python train.py' afterwards.

Fungal infection.advice: Keep affected areas clean and dry. Antifungal creams may help. Consult a dermatologist if persistent.

Allergy.advice: Avoid known allergens. Antihistamines may relieve symptoms. See an allergist for persistent issues.

GERD.advice: Avoid spicy/acidic foods, eat smaller meals, don't lie down after eating. Consult a gastroenterologist.

Chronic cholestasis.advice: Requires medical evaluation. See a hepatologist promptly.

Drug Reaction.advice: Stop the suspected medication immediately and seek emergency medical care.

Peptic ulcer disease.advice: Avoid NSAIDs and alcohol. See a gastroenterologist for proper diagnosis and treatment.

AIDS.advice: Seek immediate medical care. Antiretroviral therapy can manage the condition effectively.

Diabetes.advice: Monitor blood sugar levels, maintain a healthy diet and exercise. See an endocrinologist.

Gastroenteritis.advice: Stay hydrated. Rest and allow the illness to pass. Seek care if symptoms worsen.

Bronchial Asthma.advice: Identify and avoid triggers. Use prescribed inhalers. Have an action plan for attacks.

Hypertension.advice: Reduce salt intake, exercise regularly, manage stress. Monitor BP and consult a cardiologist.

Migraine.advice: Rest in a dark quiet room. Stay hydrated. Consult a neurologist for recurring migraines.

Cervical spondylosis.advice: Physical therapy and neck exercises can help. Consult an orthopedic specialist.

Paralysis (brain hemorrhage).advice: EMERGENCY: Call 911 immediately. This requires urgent hospital care.

Jaundice.advice: Seek immediate medical evaluation to determine the underlying cause.

Malaria.advice: Seek immediate medical treatment. Antimalarial medications must be prescribed by a doctor.

Chicken pox.advice: Rest, stay hydrated, avoid scratching. Keep away from vulnerable individuals.

Dengue.advice: Stay hydrated, rest, take acetaminophen (NOT ibuprofen). Seek hospital care for severe cases.

Typhoid.advice: Requires antibiotic treatment prescribed by a doctor. Maintain strict hygiene.

Hepatitis A.advice: Rest and stay hydrated. Most cases resolve on their own. See a doctor.

Hepatitis B.advice: Consult a hepatologist. Antiviral medications may be needed. Vaccination available.

Hepatitis C.advice: Modern antiviral treatments are very effective. Consult a hepatologist promptly.

Hepatitis D.advice: Only occurs with Hepatitis B. Requires specialized medical treatment.

Hepatitis E.advice: Mostly self-limiting but can be severe in pregnant women. Seek medical care.

Alcoholic hepatitis.advice: Stop alcohol consumption immediately. Seek urgent medical care.

Tuberculosis.advice: Requires a strict course of antibiotics for 6+ months. See a pulmonologist immediately.

Common Cold.advice: Rest, stay hydrated, and use OTC medications for symptom relief. Wash hands frequently.

Pneumonia.advice: Seek medical care promptly. May require antibiotics or hospitalization.

Dimorphic hemorrhoids (piles).advice: High-fiber diet, adequate hydration. Consult a gastroenterologist for treatment options.

Heart attack.advice: EMERGENCY: Call 911 immediately. Every second counts.

Varicose veins.advice: Elevate legs, wear compression stockings. Consult a vascular specialist for treatment options.

Hypothyroidism.advice: Hormone replacement therapy is effective. Regular monitoring by an endocrinologist needed.

Hyperthyroidism.advice: Medications, radioiodine therapy, or surgery may be needed. See an endocrinologist.

Hypoglycemia.advice: Consume fast-acting sugar immediately. Monitor blood glucose and consult a doctor.

Osteoarthritis.advice: Exercise, weight management, and physical therapy help. Consult a rheumatologist.

Arthritis.advice: Anti-inflammatory medications and physical therapy can help. See a rheumatologist.

Vertigo (Paroxysmal Positional Vertigo).advice: Epley maneuver may help. Consult an ENT specialist for diagnosis.

Acne.advice: Keep skin clean, avoid touching face. Consult a dermatologist for persistent acne.

Urinary tract infection.advice: Drink plenty of water. Antibiotics are usually required — see a doctor.

Psoriasis.advice: Moisturize regularly. Consult a dermatologist for treatment options including topical and systemic therapies.

Impetigo.advice: Antibiotic cream or oral antibiotics required. Keep area clean and avoid spreading.
